name: Martinique (France, 2019-2021)

colors.symbolism: the steep hills (mornes) and lush nature of the island
the Atlantic Ocean and the Caribbean Sea

description: A field of eight alternating green and blue segments formed by white horizontal, vertical and diagonal dividing lines; at the centre a white disc bearing a blue lambi conch, encircled by 34 small Amerindian stars and a thin ring of eight arc segments, both alternating green and blue.
history: Named Ipséité ('selfhood') and designed by Johnny Vigne, the flag won the competition launched in 2018 by the Collectivité Territoriale de Martinique for a flag to represent the island at international sporting and cultural events. It was officially presented on 10 May 2019 by executive-council president Alfred Marie-Jeanne and first flown at the 2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup. On 15 November 2021 the administrative tribunal annulled the flag and anthem, ruling that their selection had not been within the Executive Council's powers; the design remains in use as a cultural symbol. Martinique adopted the rouge-vert-noir as its official flag in 2023.

features.symbolism: the eight segments evoke eight of the languages spoken on the island: French, Creole, English, Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, Chinese and Arabic
an emblematic shell of the Antilles, whose conch is used as a traditional musical instrument

features.role: 34 Amerindian stars around the central disc, alternating green and blue
